title: Reasons for Beethoven
wikitext:
[]: Reasons for Beethoven             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Tue Mar 22, 2016 6:54 pm Aside from the obvious irony of playing joyous classical music over a sci-fi disaster scene, what explanations can you guys imagine that are reasons for the 9th symphonies use in Q?

[]: Re: Reasons for Beethoven             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Mar 22, 2016 7:44 pm Because it was used in NGE. And in NGE, classical music in general fit rather well for multiple reasons. From having religious context and aesthetic, to fitting with themes of Instrumentality and personal enlightenment, and lending itself to the deconstruction of the medium of animation.

[]: Re: Reasons for Beethoven             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 22, 2016 7:47 pm Interestingly, Ode to Joy is associated in Japan with the turning of the year . This might have some straightforward symbolic connotations in Eva , but I'll leave the massaging of this angle to others.

[]: Re: Reasons for Beethoven             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Mar 22, 2016 8:01 pm Reasons for Beethoven: 1. Beethoven 2. See #1. In all honesty, the way the lyrics in Beethoven's 9th play interestingly with the rest of the choral lyrics in the ret of the musical score. Rei's introduction has music that sings of God's creation, while AAA Wunder's first flight is hailed with a choir singing of Humanity's strength. Beethoven's 9th sings of Humanity discovering enlightenment through finding Divinity, whereas the song directly following it sings of humanity terrified of God's wrath.

[]: Re: Reasons for Beethoven             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Sun Mar 27, 2016 5:02 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Beethoven's 9th sings of Humanity discovering enlightenment through finding Divinity

[]: Re: Reasons for Beethoven             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Mar 27, 2016 9:23 pm ^ I believe it does. Whereas the song immediately following it seems to be more about the fear of this plan from Wille's perspective.

[]: Re: Reasons for Beethoven             Quote Post by Didi » Thu Sep 01, 2016 5:03 pm I just read the "Ode to Joy" (used as chorus of Beethoven's 9th Symphony) entry on wikipedia. It tells us about a bit about how the Ode to Joy's poet thought about the Ode to Joy, and I think it's quite parallel to Shinji and Kaworu's circumstances. Despite the lasting popularity of the ode, Schiller himself regarded it as a failure later in his life, going so far as calling it " detached from reality " and " of value maybe for us two, but not for the world , nor for the art of poetry" in an 1800 letter to his long-time friend and patron Christian Gottfried Körner (whose friendship had originally inspired him to write the ode)
summary:
[]: Reasons for Beethoven             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Tue Mar 22, 2016 6:54 pm Aside from the obvious irony of playing joyous classical music over a sci-fi disaster scene, what explanations can you guys imagine that are reasons for the 9th symphonies use in Q?

[]: Re: Reasons for Beethoven             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Mar 22, 2016 7:44 pm Because it was used
